meaning_english: Sharp / Bitter. Example: Acrimonious (bitter speech), Acerbic (sharp or sour in manner).
Both / Around. Example: Amphitheatre (circular theater), Amphibian (lives on both land and water).
About / To make. Example: Belittle (to make small), Bemoan (to complain about).
War. Example: Belligerent (hostile), Antebellum (before the war).
Go / Yield. Example: Concede (yield a point), Recession (going backward).
Know. Example: Cognizant (being aware), Incognito (disguised/unknown).
Body. Example: Corporeal (physical body), Corporate (body of people).
Believe. Example: Credulous (ready to believe), Credence (belief in something).
Sleep. Example: Dormant (inactive/sleeping), Dormitory (sleeping room).
Make / Do. Example: Facsimile (an exact copy), Artifact (object made by skill).
Birth / Race. Example: Genesis (origin), Progeny (offspring).
Throw. Example: Conjecture (guess thrown together), Dejected (sad/thrown down).
Great / Large. Example: Magnate (powerful person), Magnanimous (generous/great soul).
Bad / Evil. Example: Malevolent (wishing evil), Malady (illness).
Send. Example: Remit (send back money), Emission (sending out).
Change. Example: Immutable (unchangeable), Mutable (variable).
New. Example: Novelty (newness), Renovate (make new again).
Peace. Example: Pacify (calm down), Appease (satisfy to make peace).
Father. Example: Paternal (fatherly), Patriarch (male head).
Foot. Example: Pedestrian (walker), Chiropodist (foot doctor).
Carry. Example: Portfolio (case for carrying papers), Export (carry out).
Rule / Straight. Example: Regal (royal), Rectify (make straight/correct).
Break / Burst. Example: Bankruptcy (broken bank), Abrupt (sudden break).
Cut. Example: Bisect (cut into two), Intersection (cutting across).
Alone. Example: Soliloquy (speaking alone), Desolate (lonely).
Look / See. Example: Retrospect (looking back), Perspicacious (sharp-sighted).
Build. Example: Infrastructure (foundation), Construe (interpret/build meaning).
Pull. Example: Protract (lengthen), Tractable (easy to manage/pull).
Come. Example: Convene (come together), Advent (arrival).
Voice / Call. Example: Evoke (call out), Equivocal (uncertain voice).
